metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Male
Moderate Agreeableness (42nd pct)
Moderate Conscientiousness (48th pct)
Highly introverted Extraversion (19th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Anxious Neuroticism (76th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly open Openness to Experience (82nd pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Moderate Tender-Mindedness (46th pct)
  Moderate Achievement Striving (55th pct)
  Low Self-Discipline (34th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  Low Activity Level (36th pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Low Excitement Seeking (23rd pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (1st pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  High Anger / Hostility (75th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (100th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (80th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Moderate Chronological Age (59th pct)

message.reasoning:
We are simulating a baseline opinion for a specific person on "Trust In Mainstream Journalism". The profile is a 59-year-old male with very high neuroticism (anxiety, anger, self-consciousness), very low extraversion (especially low assertiveness, gregariousness, positive emotions), high openness to experience, moderate agreeableness (trust facet is moderately high at 57.6, but compliance is low at 29.5, modesty low at 30.3, altruism moderate at 40.5). He has low extraversion and low positive emotions, high neuroticism. He is likely to be skeptical, anxious, and possibly distrustful of institutions due to high anxiety and anger/hostility. However, his openness to experience is high (82nd pct), which may make him more receptive to new ideas but also critical. He is older (59) which might correlate with traditional media trust, but his high neuroticism and low positive emotions might lead to general distrust. He has moderate trust (57.6) on agreeableness trust facet, which suggests he is not deeply suspicious but somewhat trusting. But his low compliance (29.5) and high self-consciousness (99.7) might make him more independent. Also, his high openness to ideas (79.8) and values (81.1) suggests he is open to questioning. We need to produce a score on trust in mainstream journalism.

Given his high neuroticism and low extraversion, he may be more prone to worry about misinformation or bias. But his trust facet is moderate-high, so he might have a baseline moderate trust. However, his high anger/hostility could make him critical of media. I think he would lean slightly negative but not extreme. He might be around -200 to -400. Not a maximal distrust because his openness might make him appreciate some journalism.

Let's aim for -315 as a fine-grained number. Confidence moderate around 0.7.

Reasoning: The profile combines moderate trust (57.6 pct) with high neuroticism and low extraversion. The anxiety and hostility may tilt toward skepticism of mainstream outlets, but openness and moderate trust prevent extreme distrust. Hence mild opposition.
